character.name: Elena
character.summary: Elena is a single mother working two jobs. When her daughter arrived at school wearing unwashed clothes for several consecutive days, a teacher made a report.

intro.title: Poverty Mistaken for Neglect
intro.description: The child welfare system often misinterprets symptoms of poverty—such as crowded housing or unwashed clothing—as parental "neglect." Elena's experience highlights the alternative CARES track.

blocks.title: The School's Call
blocks.body: Elena and her daughter were recently evicted and had been sleeping on a friend's couch. Without access to a laundromat or extra money, washing clothes became impossible. A teacher, acting as a "mandated reporter," called the hotline.

blocks.title: Accepted for Assessment
blocks.body: ACS accepted the report, classifying the dirty clothing as a potential indicator of deeper "educational and physical neglect," and dispatched a worker.

blocks.title: The Home Visit
blocks.body: A caseworker arrived at the friend's apartment. Despite the cramped living conditions, the worker quickly determined the child was unharmed, fed, and clearly loved.

blocks.text: I am not neglecting my child. I just didn't have the $10 needed for the laundromat this week.

blocks.title: Diverted to CARES
blocks.body: Because there was no immediate danger, Elena's case was diverted to the CARES (Collaborative Assessment, Response, Engagement & Support) track. No formal investigation was launched, and she avoided a permanent record. The worker helped connect Elena to a local food pantry and a temporary laundry stipend.

blocks.text: While the CARES track avoids the stigma of a formal record, it still subjects families to systemic surveillance in exchange for basic material needs.

ending.title: A Temporary Relief
ending.description: Sixty days later, the CARES case was closed. Elena received some short-term material help, but her fundamental housing instability remained unresolved. Her story asks: why must families enter the 'child protection' system just to access basic social safety nets?
actions: Support direct cash assistance and housing subsidies for families in poverty.
Reduce reliance on mandated reporters by funding community-based mutual aid.
